title: trapa bicornis var. jinumae
intro: Trapa bicornis var. jinumae, also known as the Jinuma water chestnut or Japanese water chestnut, is a variety of the water chestnut plant native to Japan. It is a type of aquatic plant that grows in the water and is rich in nutrients, including starch, protein, and minerals. The medicinal properties of Trapa bicornis var. jinumae include its ability to treat various health conditions, such as fever, diarrhea, and inflammation, due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional herbal medicine, it is often prepared as a powder or tea, and its health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
